methodsAttempted.query: site:plymouth.ac.uk "Please do not upload personal" "AI tools"
methodsAttempted.notes: Public web search surfaced Education Commons generative-AI pages, but both Firecrawl scrape and local curl failed DNS resolution for ec.plymouth.ac.uk during this run.
The PDF host returned HTTP 200 for the AI assessment PDF. /robots.txt returned 404, so no disallow rule was found for the directly discovered PDF.
Official Plymouth governance pages for information security and student privacy were reachable, but they were generic data/security pages and did not provide AI-specific policy evidence.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found one directly fetchable Plymouth-hosted AI assessment guidance PDF for the Faculty of Science and Engineering. Broader Education Commons AI pages were discovered but were inaccessible due to DNS resolution failures during this run. PEARL repository research and generic governance pages were documented but rejected for policy specificity or source-scope reasons. Claims are limited to the Faculty of Science and Engineering assessment guidance and do not assert a central university-wide AI policy.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Assessments

artifacts.claimText: University of Plymouth Faculty of Science and Engineering guidance says AI tools such as ChatGPT should generally not be used to generate the final version of assessed work, unless the coursework briefing clearly states an exception.

artifacts.title: University of Plymouth AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Draft evidence set for University of Plymouth. The only verified claim source in this run is a Plymouth-hosted Faculty of Science and Engineering PDF on responsible AI use in assessments. The draft records three faculty-scoped claims on final submitted work, limited planning/support uses, and ChatGPT-generated assessed work as plagiarism. Education Commons AI pages were discovered but inaccessible due to DNS resolution failures, so they were rejected for this run rather than used as evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are scoped to the Faculty of Science and Engineering guidance and should not be read as a central university-wide AI policy.
Education Commons AI pages were discoverable but not fetchable during this run because ec.plymouth.ac.uk failed DNS resolution.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
